$comment: Disclosure-envelope wire-format test vectors for parameters_disclosure as pinned by ADR-0012 (envelope canonical-shape amendment) and the sibling schema spec/schema/parameters-disclosure.schema.json. Each vector pins the canonical envelope *shape* (field set, key order, encoding rules, alg string) that the hub re-verifier and the three SDKs (Go, TS, Py) MUST produce. Vectors with `computed_values_status: "placeholder"` have the shape pinned but the concrete `enc`/`ct` bytes not yet filled in — those are computed by the first SDK to ship the HPKE primitive and added in a follow-up PR. Once `computed_values_status` is `"computed"`, the vector also pins the exact byte values given (a) a plaintext parameters object, (b) a recipient X25519 public key (kid + raw key bytes), and (c) a deterministic HPKE ephemeral-key seed (`ikmE`, RFC 9180 §7.1.3 DeriveKeyPair). Wiring into SDK test suites is a later track. See the companion README.md for the seed pattern and key provenance.

encoding.binary_fields: unpadded base64url (RFC 4648 §5), matching ADR-0009 / spec §4 proofValue convention
encoding.plaintext_canonicalisation: RFC 8785 JCS over the parameters object before AEAD encryption

test_recipients.name: forensic-test-recipient-1
test_recipients.$comment: Well-known X25519 test key. Public key matches RFC 7748 §6.1's Alice public-key example. Use only for spec test vectors; never as a real forensic key.
test_recipients.private_key_source: Alice secret key from RFC 7748 §6.1 (https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7748#section-6.1). Reviewers running the decryption round-trip fetch the private key from the RFC directly; it is deliberately not inlined here to avoid tripping secret-scanning tooling on raw key hex regardless of provenance.

test_recipients.name: forensic-test-recipient-2
test_recipients.$comment: Well-known X25519 test key. Public key matches RFC 7748 §6.1's Bob public-key example.
test_recipients.private_key_source: Bob secret key from RFC 7748 §6.1 (https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7748#section-6.1). Same not-inlined-here pattern as recipient-1.

vectors.name: vector-1-single-recipient-small-payload
vectors.description: Smallest realistic disclosure: a single string parameter. Demonstrates the envelope shape end-to-end with the forensic-test-recipient-1 key and the RFC 9180 §A.1.1 ephemeral seed.

hpke_ephemeral.ikmE_source: RFC 9180 §A.1.1 (DHKEM(X25519, HKDF-SHA256) base-mode test vector). The ephemeral key pair is deterministically derived from ikmE via HPKE DeriveKeyPair; using the RFC's published seed lets implementers cross-check the encapsulated key against RFC 9180 directly.

round_trip.decrypt_with: Alice secret key per RFC 7748 §6.1. The correct 32-byte hex scalar is 77076d0a7318a57d3c16c17251b26645df4c2f87ebc0992ab177fba51db92c2a (verified: X25519(scalar, basepoint) = alice_pub above). The RFC's hex presentation uses a line-break convention that can be misread; this pinned value is authoritative for this vector.

vectors.computed_values_note: Values computed by the Go SDK (github.com/agent-receipts/ar/sdk/go) using github.com/cloudflare/circl/hpke v1.6.3, ciphersuite KEM_X25519_HKDF_SHA256 / KDF_HKDF_SHA256 / AEAD_AES256GCM. The ikmE bytes are fed to circl's Sender.Setup(io.Reader); circl internally applies DHKEM(X25519) DeriveKeyPair (RFC 9180 §4.1) — HKDF over ikmE — to derive the ephemeral scalar skEm, then computes enc = pkEm = X25519(skEm, basepoint). This is confirmed by enc = RFC 9180 §A.1.1 pkEm (37fda3... = N_2jVnvb1ijohmjDyNfpfR0SU7bU6m1EwVD3QfG_RDE). Other SDKs implementing this deterministic path MUST apply DeriveKeyPair(ikmE) and NOT use ikmE directly as the scalar. Plaintext before encryption = RFC 8785 JCS of the plaintext_object. Decryption with Alice's private key recovers the expected plaintext. See sdk/go/receipt/disclosure_test.go:TestDeterministicVector1.

vectors.name: vector-2-single-recipient-nested-payload
vectors.description: Nested object plaintext to exercise JCS over the parameters before encryption. Different recipient so the kid field's fingerprint form is also covered.

hpke_ephemeral.ikmE_source: Deterministic per-vector seed for cross-SDK reproducibility. Not from any external RFC — fixed in this file so all three SDKs produce identical envelopes for vector-2. Generated by SHA-256("agent-receipts/spec/test-vectors/disclosure-envelope/vector-2-ikmE-v1").

round_trip.decrypt_with: Bob secret key per RFC 7748 §6.1: 5dab087e624a8a4b79e17f8b83800ee66f3bb1292618b6fd1c2f8b27ff88e0eb (verified: X25519(scalar, basepoint) = bob_pub above).

vectors.computed_values_note: Values computed by the Go SDK (github.com/agent-receipts/ar/sdk/go) using github.com/cloudflare/circl/hpke v1.6.3. Same approach as vector-1. Plaintext before encryption = RFC 8785 JCS of the nested plaintext_object (key order: body, headers, method). Decryption with Bob's private key recovers the expected plaintext. See sdk/go/receipt/disclosure_test.go:TestDeterministicVector2.

shape_invariants.$comment: Properties every conformant SDK MUST hold over its produced envelopes. These are what the cross-SDK harness asserts against SDK output. They do NOT apply to the angle-bracket placeholder strings (`<enc-bytes-...>`, `<ciphertext-bytes-...>`) in the test vectors above — those placeholders are deliberately invalid base64url so a copy-paste into production fails loudly. A vector's `computed_values_status` field flips from `"placeholder"` to `"computed"` once a follow-up PR replaces the placeholders with SDK-computed bytes; from that point the vector is also expected to satisfy these rules and validate against `parameters-disclosure.schema.json`.
rules: RFC 8785 JCS over the envelope object sorts top-level keys as [alg, ct, recipients, v].
Inside each recipients[] entry, JCS sorts keys as [enc, kid].
`v` MUST be the JSON string "1", not the JSON number 1.
`alg` MUST be exactly the string "hpke-x25519-hkdf-sha256-aes-256-gcm" — no whitespace, no case variation, no version suffix.
`recipients` MUST be a JSON array of length 1 in v1. Length 0 is invalid; length >1 is reserved for a v2 multi-recipient envelope and MUST NOT appear with `v: "1"`.

`enc` MUST be exactly 43 unpadded-base64url characters (the encoding of a 32-byte X25519 public key). `ct` MUST be unpadded base64url with at least 24 characters (v1 plaintext is RFC 8785 canonical JSON of a parameters object, minimum `{}` = 2 bytes; 2 + 16-byte GCM tag = 18 bytes = 24 base64url chars). Neither field carries `=` padding, `+`, or `/`.
No `nonce` field is present in v1 envelopes. HPKE single-shot derives the AEAD nonce from the KEM output internally; surfacing a redundant `nonce` field would be a v1 schema violation.

The HPKE `info` parameter is the empty string. The HPKE AAD is the empty string. v1 does not bind any out-of-band context into encryption; the surrounding signed receipt envelope already authenticates the disclosure field via its signature.
Plaintext is RFC 8785 JCS over the parameters object as a JSON object. Strings, numbers, arrays at the top level are not supported as plaintext in v1; wrap in an object.
